pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly downloads and configures PDB ID 1CLL from rcsb.org.
Export script and verifier.py logic explicitly use this file path.
Matches explicit target paths in task.json instructions and export_result.sh checks.
Matches explicit target paths in task.json instructions and export_result.sh checks.
Matches explicit target paths in task.json instructions and export_result.sh checks.
Explicit minimum threshold enforced by the verifier logic.

Independent geometric calculation of CA-CA intra-chain pairwise distances (< 8.0 Å, separation > 12) from the official 1CLL.pdb downloaded file yields exactly 58 contacts, contradicting the required 100 minimum threshold.
Domain contact density analysis of 1CLL confirms the central linker domain begins roughly between residues 72-74, well within the target bound.
Domain contact density analysis of 1CLL confirms the C-terminal domain begins roughly at residue 85, placing it within the target bound.
The ground truth exact contact count of 58 successfully falls within this target bound for the report textual search.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real, non-synthetic 1CLL Calmodulin PDB file downloaded from rcsb.org. Independent geometric calculation of the required Cα-Cα distances (< 8.0 Å, sequence separation > 12) on 1CLL yields precisely 58 true long-range contacts. This identifies a critical contradiction: the verifier's explicit 'min_contacts' threshold strictly requires an output of 100 contacts, causing an honest and correct agent solution (58 contacts) to fail the file-contents test. The target domain boundaries checked by the verifier ([60, 80] for the N-terminal/linker and [85, 100] for the linker/C-terminal) accurately bound the true biological domains of Calmodulin. The true contact count (58) cleanly passes the `plausible_contact_count` array bounds [50, 600] applied when scanning the agent's textual report.
